info.title: Business Process API Documentation
info.description:
Business Process API is a REST API which allows you to configure your instance and integrate it with your information system or external apps.

# Overview

## Common responses

The API uses standard HTTP status codes to indicate the success or failure of the API call. This documentation details for each method the format for responses. However, some format are common to several responses. This section covers those cases.

### Creations

All resource creations use the same format:
```
{
  "id": "1",
  "location": "https://r12345-euw1-apigateway.3dexperience.3ds.com/api/businessprocess/v2/path/to/resource/1"
}
```
The `id` can be a string or an integer depending on the context.
The `location` is the URL corresponding to the resource created.

The response will also have a `location` header, containing the same URL.


### Errors

In case of failure (HTTP status codes over 400), the body of the response will always use the following JSON format:
```
{
  "code": 499,
  "message": "This particular error occurred. Try that."
}
```

get.description: Fetch global history process data and variables.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.description: Process instance id of the process

parameters.name: variableFilter
parameters.description: List of variables ids to recover if getVariables=true, if not specified all will be returned. Use comma as separator.

parameters.name: taskFilter
parameters.description: List of task key to recover. If getTask=true all task will be fetch if not specified

403.description: You do not have right to view this process history.

404.description: History process instance not found.

get.summary: Retrieve all process instances information related to a given 3DX object id
get.description: Associate a 3DX object to a process instance to be able to retrieve all process instances related to a 3DX object afterward.

400.description: Provided 3DX object id is null.

403.description: You do not have enough right to perform this action or you should not perform this action as robot.

get.summary: Fetch process instance history by identificator
get.description: Fetch process data for a given identificator instance, and an optional processKey. If several processes match conditions, all will be returned.

parameters.name: strict
parameters.description: If false search identificator that contains the argument, rather than a strict equality.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Process state: COMPLETED, WAITING or DELETED

403.description: You do not have right to view this process history.

404.description: Unknown processKey.

post.description:
Search for specific process instances using process keys. You can choose variable to retrieve its value.
The body request must contain `processFilters` with at least one `processKey`
User Rights: at least supervisor of each key requested

403.description: You do not have enough right to perform this action.

post.description:
Search for specific values from processes table using value selectors. You can choose the columns to retrieve or get them all. The body request must contain a list of exactly one object as `or` filters are not yet implemented.
Note: by default, only in progress processes are retrieved. Please add a process filter on the `state` property to change this default behavior.

parameters.name: variableIds
parameters.description: Ids of the variables to retrieve. Ex: `start_date,start_user`

400.description: Bad request.

403.description: You do not have rights to view this process data.

404.description: Process or variable not found.

post.summary: Set a 3DX object id to a process instance
post.description: Associate a 3DX object to a process instance to be able to retrieve all process instances related to a 3DX object afterward.

200.description: 3DX object successfully associated to process instance.

400.description: Process instance doesn't exist or provided 3DX object id is null.

403.description: You do not have enough right to perform this action.

404.description: Process instance not found.

get.description: Fetch user task instances history for the past X days where X is 30 by default. Only takes into account tasks that were finished by the user.

parameters.name: getVariables
parameters.description: Recover tasks variables alongside tasks history

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Number of whole days of history to take into account (ex: setting `days=0` will get you all tasks of current day, starting 00:00)

parameters.name: login
parameters.description: User to get the task history from, in case this method is called by a robot

400.description: Bad request.

404.description: User not found.

post.summary: Search for a task by process and task filters
post.description:
Return the task information about a task based on the process and task filters.
User rights:
Either- at least (dynamic) supervisor of the process/ user is or was actor of the task.

parameters.name: withProvidedData
parameters.description: Set `withProvidedData` value `true` to get the provided data to the task. Default value is `false`

parameters.name: withExpectedFields
parameters.description: Set `withExpectedFields` value `true` to get the expected fields of the task. Default value is `false`

400.description: Bad request.

404.description: Task / Process not found

delete.description:
Deletes a deployed process and all current and historical instances for a given process key. The user should have access to Design and have the rights to see the process to be deleted.
Deletes all the versions of the process, by default, unless a specific version is provided. In that case, only the given version and associated history will be deleted.
The last deployed model is kept as a model, by default, except if 'keepAsModel' is set to false.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force deletion of historic or active processes.

parameters.name: keepAsModel
parameters.description: Keep the last deployed process as a model in version 1.Ignored if the query parameter 'version' is provided and different than the current deployed version.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: If specified, only delete a specific version of the process. Will delete all versions otherwise.

400.description: Expected non null user or the process is already deployed.

403.description: You don't have rights on this process.

404.description: Expected a user for the given login or no undeployed process found for the given process key.

409.description: Existing historic or running processes instances for the process or version. Use force parameter to force deletion.

delete.description: Deletes an undeployed process model for a given process key. The user should have access to Design and have the rights to see the model to be deleted.

400.description: Expected non null user or the process is already deployed.

403.description: You don't have rights on this process.

404.description: Expected a user for the given login or no undeployed process found for the given process key.

post.summary: Import process model into Business Process service
post.description:
Import process model into Business Process service.
You can also simulate the import by only performing checks on given bpmn file (see the `dryRun` boolean input).
If the process key is already used, a new one will be generated.
A process having a process key starting with a number will not be imported.

200.description: List of messages (`dryRun` boolean input set to `true` only).

201.description: Process started (`dryRun` boolean input undefined or set to `false` only).

400.description: Process Key cannot start with a number.

403.description: You do not have rights to import on this process key.

409.description: Errors/Warnings found while trying to import.

post.summary: [BETA] Full import process model into Business Process service
post.description:
Import process model into Business Process service with all its dependencies.
You can also simulate the import by only performing checks on given bpmn file (see the `dryRun` boolean input).
If the processes keys are already used, imported processes will be merged as new version of the existing processes.
A process having a process key starting with a number will not be imported.

200.description: List of messages (`dryRun` boolean input set to `true` only).

201.description: Process started (`dryRun` boolean input undefined or set to `false` only).

400.description: Process Key cannot start with a number.

403.description: You do not have rights to import on this process key.

409.description: Errors/Warnings found while trying to import.

parameters.description: List of variable ids to retrieved, separated by '+'. If not set all variables will be retrieved.

put.summary: Start a process with file attached
put.description:
Start a new process instance, using provided input values and uploading provided files. The request must be sent as a form-data format with following keys:
- `body` for the starting variables.
- the corresponding variable id for each file to upload (can be more than one). Example: a `start_myFileId` part containing a binary file will create the variable `start_myFileId` in the process with the uploaded file. Please check that the file metadata are send in UTF-8 or avoid any special characters in its name (specially on Windows or MacOS).

400.description: No file found in request.

403.description: You do not have right to start this process.

404.description: Process unknown. Check provided processKey.

post.description: Start a new process instance, using provided input values.

403.description: You do not have right to start this process.

404.description: Process unknown. Check provided processKey.

patch.description: Either interrupt, complete or delete a process instance by id.

put.summary: Update process instance variable with file attached
put.description:
Update process instance variable with possibility to update file variable.The request must be sent as a form-data format with following keys:
1)Body for the variable that are not files. JSON part made of key values.
2)The corresponding variable id for each file to upload (can be more than one).
Example: a start_myFileId part containing a binary file will update the variable start_myFileId in the process with the uploaded file.
Please check that the file metadata are sent in UTF-8 or avoid any special characters in it's name (specially on Windows or MacOS).

404.description: Process unknown.

403.description: User not authorized.

400.description: Variable map and files both are empty.

endTime.description: End time of the process (timestamp), empty if process in progress

endDate.description: Task end time (timestamp), empty if task not ended

endTime.description: End time of the process (timestamp), empty if process in progress
End time of the process (timestamp), empty if process in progress

priority.description:
Priority of the process as `Integer`.
The possible values are:
- 1: urgent priority
- 0: normal priority

startDate.description: Process start date as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

endDate.description: Process end date as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

deleteReason.description: Reason given by the user when interrupting the process

priority.description:
Priority of the process as `Integer`.
The possible values are:
- 1: urgent priority
- 0: normal priority

state.description: State of the process to get as `Text` value. Possible values are: `finished`, `unfinished`

endDateFrom.description: Process end date (from), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

endDateTo.description: Process end date (to), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

startDateFrom.description: Process start date (from), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

startDateTo.description: Process start date (to), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

processKeys.description: List of Process Keys to search the process

lastUpdatedFrom.description: Process last upadted date (from), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

lastUpdatedTo.description: Process last upadted date (to), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

variableIdsToRetrieve.description: List of variable ids to retrieve

headers.description: Map of id_header to headers name.

processFilters.description:
Condition filters on process data. All conditions must be met for a process instance to be returned (alongside optional ones set on process variables, see below).

Here is the exhaustive list of all possible filters, all of them being optional ones:
  • `identificatorInstance`: process identifier as TEXT. Only processes whose process identifier contains the provided input will be fetched.
  • `state`: state of the processes to get as `Text` value. Possible values are: `all`, `current`, `finished`, `deleted`, `not_current`, `not_finished` and `not_deleted`. Default value is `current`.
  • `priority`: priority of the process as `Integer`.
  • `inProgressTaskName`: name or part of task name as `String`. Only processes having user task currently in progress and whose name contains provided input will be fetched.
  • `startDateFrom`: date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond). If provided without `startDateTo`, get all processes started from provided date to current date.
  • `startDateTo`: date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond). If provided without `startDateFrom`, get all processes started from big bang date to provided date.
  • `endDateFrom`: date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond). If provided without `endDateTo`, get all processes ended from provided date to current date.
  • `endDateTo`: date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond). If provided without `endDateFrom`, get all processes ended from big bang date to provided date.
  • `sortAscending`: `Boolean`. If set to `true`, the results will be presented in ascending order of the parameter `sortedColumn`. If set to `false`, the results will be presented in decreasing order of the parameter `sortedColumn`. Default value is `false`.
  • `sortedColumn`: id of the column as `Text` you want the results sorted by. The id must be one of the variable ids specified in the query parameters or one of the default process column id from this list: `c_controls_state_ui`, `identificator`, `priority`, `c_controls_duration`, `c_controls_progress`, `c_controls_startDate`, `c_controls_endDate`, `c_controls_creator`.Default value is `c_controls_startDate`.
  • `updatedFrom`: date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond). Get all processes having any activity instance end time ranging between `updatedFrom` and `updatedTo` or processes having any one of the following event changes PROCESS_INSTANCE_STOP_INTERRUPT PROCESS_INSTANCE_STOP_TERMINATE PROCESS_INSTANCE_DELETE_HISTORY VARIABLE_CHANGE VARIABLE_CHANGE_INSIDE_SCRIPT after the `updatedFrom` date
  • `updatedTo`: date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond). Get all processes having any activity instance end time ranging between `updatedFrom` and `updatedTo` or processes having any one of the following event changes PROCESS_INSTANCE_STOP_INTERRUPT PROCESS_INSTANCE_STOP_TERMINATE PROCESS_INSTANCE_DELETE_HISTORY VARIABLE_CHANGE VARIABLE_CHANGE_INSIDE_SCRIPT before the `updatedTo` date

endDate.description: Task end time (timestamp), empty if task not ended

actor.description: Actor of the task, empty if task not ended or not human

processInformation.description: Process information the task belongs to

description.example: This is what you have to know about this variable.

parameter.description: Is variable a start process parameter

values.description: Possible values for list selection (simple or multiple), separated by '##'.

defaultValue.description: Default value filled in the form.

min.description:
Low limit for the variable. It has a different meaning depending on the type of data.
For numbers, it is the minimum accepted value.
For texts, it is the minimum number of characters.
For dates, this limit can be a number, in this case, it corresponds to the number of days in regard to the day of the form validation. For example if the min is '-1', the minimum date can be yesterday.
This value can be a string too. In this case it corresponds to a fixed date using one of the following format: "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mmZ" or "dd/MM/yy".
For all other type, this field has no meaning.

max.description: High limit for the variable. See `min` definition for detail

regex.description: Expected format, expressed using regular expression

composedVariableDefinition.description: Formalized definition of a composed variable, only metadata, not values

value.description: Variable value. Can be any type.

identificatorInstance.description: Identificator Instance as `Text` (At least one of the 3 parameters (Process Key/ Process Instance ID/ Identificator Instance) required)

priority.description:
Priority of the process as `Integer`.
The possible values are:
- 1: urgent priority
- 0: normal priority

state.description: State of the process to get as `Text` value. Possible values are: `finished`, `unfinished`

endDateFrom.description: Process end date (from), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

endDateTo.description: Process end date (to), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

startDateFrom.description: Process start date (from), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

startDateTo.description: Process start date (to), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

processKey.description: Process Key as `Text` (At least one of the 3 parameters (Process Key/ Process Instance ID/ Identificator Instance) required)

processInstanceId.description: Process Instance ID as `Text` (At least one of the 3 parameters (Process Key/ Process Instance ID/ Identificator Instance) required)

state.description: State of the task to get as `Text` value. Possible values are: `finished`, `unfinished`

endDateFrom.description: Task end date (from), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

endDateTo.description: Task end date (to), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

priority.description:
Priority of the task as `Integer`.The possible values are:
- 5: urgent priority
- 4: important
- 3: normal
- 2: minor
- 1: not a priority
- 0: the task is considered a background task

startDateFrom.description: Task start date (from), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

startDateTo.description: Task start date (to), date provided as a `Timestamp` (in millisecond).

description.description: Group description (put `""` to set the database value back to `null`)

lastConnection.description: returns the timestamp (millisecond) of the date.

values.description: Row values. Each column refers to a property in each row of the provided array.

rows.description: List of business table rows in the form {"tableColumnName": tableColumnValue}

values.description: Row values. Each column refers to a property in each row of the provided array.

filters.description: Row condition filters. All conditions must be met for a row to be returned.

rows.description: Row values. Each column refers to a property in each row of the provided array.

selectors.description: Filter condition for table to Update. All conditions must be met for table to be updated.

newValues.description: New values for table to Update.

rowsToRemove.description: Filter condition on rows to remove. All conditions must be met for a row to be deleted.

rowsToAdd.description: Row values to add. Each column refers to a property in each row of the provided array.

defaultValue.description: Value to set on rows already in the table.

columnKeysToRemove.description: Business table columns to remove. Take note all values registered in rows will be deleted for these columns

linkedElementsToAdd.description: Linked List Elements items to Add

linkedElementsToRemove.description: Linked List Elements items to Remove

position.description: Item position (0 to insert it as first item, 1 to insert it after the first, etc.)

description.description: List description (set it to an empty string in case you want to remove it)

itemsToRemove.description: List items to remove. A value appearing there and in `itemsWithPositionToAdd` or `itemsToAdd` will still be added to the list.

itemsWithPositionToAdd.description: List of items to add in specified position. Set an order of `0` to place it first. An item with an order `10` in a list of size 2 will be added at the end. Note: items are first removed if `itemsToRemove` is given, then added in their specified position.

itemsToAdd.description: List of items to add at the end. If both `itemsWithPositionToAdd` and `itemsToAdd` are precised, items with position will be added first then items without. A same value cannot appear twice; if so, next occurrences will be ignored.

initiatorUserLogins.description: List of `login`s to be initiators of the process. Send an empty list to remove user initiators on an already deployed process.

initiatorGroupIds.description: List of `groupId`s to be initiators of the process. Send an empty list to remove group initiators on an already deployed process.

userRoles.description: List of user's role on the process. Send an empty list to remove all user roles on an already deployed process.

groupRoles.description: List of group's role on the process. Send an empty list to remove all group roles on an already deployed process.

startableByAll.description: To be able to start the process by all. Default value is `false` on first deployment.

externalAccess.description: To allow external access on the process. Default value is `false` on first deployment.

keyDataAccessibleByLimitedSupervisors.description: To allow key data access to limited supervisor roles. Default value is `false` on first deployment.

errorNotificationType.description: Type of errorNotification. Default value is `NONE` on first deployment.

force.description: If `true`, does not return an error in case given users or groups are not found.

retentionDays.description: Period of data retention for group variables, in days. If the value specified is a positive number, it gets set as the new value for retention. If the value specified is any negative number, the retention value is set as 'always'. If the value is specified as 'null', it will take the retention value of the last deployment of the same processKey if it exists, otherwise 'always' if previous value does not exist.

endedProcessRetentionInDays.description: Period of data retention for ended processes, in days. If the value specified is a positive number, it gets set as the new value for retention. If the value specified is any negative number, the retention value is set as 'always'. If the value is specified as 'null', it will take the retention value of the last deployment of the same processKey if it exists, otherwise 'always' if previous value does not exist.

allVariablesRetentionInDays.description: Period of data retention for variables, in days. If the value specified is a positive number, it gets set as the new value for retention. If the value specified is any negative number, the retention value is set as 'always'. If the value is specified as 'null', it will take the retention value of the last deployment of the same processKey if it exists, otherwise 'always' if previous value does not exist.

filesRetentionInDays.description: Period of data retention for files, in days. If the value specified is a positive number, it gets set as the new value for retention. If the value specified is any negative number, the retention value is set as 'always'. If the value is specified as 'null', it will take the retention value of the last deployment of the same processKey if it exists, otherwise 'always' if previous value does not exist.

id.description: Login for a user, groupId for a group

description.example: To do things. Lot of things.

listsDataToExport.description: List of lists uuids which data will be exported.

dependencyTableDataToExport.description: List of linked lists uuids which data will be exported.

businessTablesDataToExport.description: List of business table repositories uuids which data will be exported.

description.example: To do things. Lot of things.

protected.description: Is process is protected with password

humanTasks.description: List of human tasks info by id.

variables.description: Map of human tasks info by id.

outputs.description: List of variable ids expected in the output form with form-specific options.

order.description: Field index in form (i.e. its position among others)

sourceUuid.description: Source uuid. can be found with the 'Retrieve all dependencies of a process' method

forceCreate.description: If a resource with the same uuid already exist, a new one will be created (with a new uuid) rather than use the existing.

targetUuid.description: Allow to specify target uuid of an existing resource to use instead of the sourceUuid.

dataManagement.description:
How to handle resource data if present into the exported file, considering that an existing resource already exist with customer data. 
 CONCAT will keep customer data and append the exported too 
 REPLACE will erase customer data and replace with the exported ones 
 CLEAR will erase customer data but will not import the exported ones 
 IDENTITY will keep customer data and will not import the exported ones

sourceId.description: Login/GroupId/Policy used in the import, and to be replaced by `id`.

targetId.description: Login/GroupId/Policy to be used in Iterop after import.

body.description: **_dryRun_** _boolean_</br>Default: ```false```</br>If set to ```true```, will return warning/errors messages you would get on import, without actually performing the importation.</br></br>**_failOnWarnings_** _boolean_</br>Default: ```true```</br>By default, process import is cancelled on errors or warnings detection. Set ```failOnWarnings``` to ```false``` if you want the import to fail only on errors detection.</br></br>**_usersMapping_** _Array of objects (Mapping)_</br>Users remapping to match logins from the imported files with specified logins.</br></br>**_groupsMapping_** _Array of objects (Mapping)_</br>Group remapping to match groupIds from the imported files with specified groupIds.</br></br>**_policiesMapping_** _Array of objects (Mapping)_</br>Policy remapping to match policy name from the imported files with specified policies name.</br></br>**_processesMapping_** _Array of objects (ProcessesMapping)_</br>Process remapping to match processKeys from the imported processes with specified processKeys.</br></br>**_listDefinitionMapping_** _Array of objects (ExternalResourcesMapping)_</br>List remapping to match list uuids from the imported processes with the target uuids and configure data management.</br></br>**_dependencyTableDefinitionMapping_** _Array of objects (ExternalResourcesMapping)_</br>Dependency table remapping to match Dependency table uuids from the imported processes with the target uuids and configure data management.</br></br>**_businessTableDefinitionMapping_** _Array of objects (ExternalResourcesMapping)_</br>Business table remapping to Business table list uuids from the imported processes with the target uuids and configure data management.

dryRun.description: If set to `true`, will return warning/errors messages you would get on import, without actually performing the importation.

failOnWarnings.description: By default, process import is cancelled on errors or warnings detection. Set `failOnWarnings` to `false` if you want the import to fail only on errors detection.

usersMapping.description: Users remapping to match logins from the imported files with specified logins.

groupsMapping.description: Group remapping to match groupIds from the imported files with specified groupIds.

policiesMapping.description: Policy remapping to match policy name from the imported files with specified policies name.

processesMapping.description: Process remapping to match processKeys from the imported processes with specified processKeys.

listDefinitionMapping.description: List remapping to match list uuids from the imported processes with the target uuids and configure data management.

dependencyTableDefinitionMapping.description: Dependency table remapping to match Dependency table uuids from the imported processes with the target uuids and configure data management.

businessTableDefinitionMapping.description: Business table remapping to Business table list uuids from the imported processes with the target uuids and configure data management.

targetProcessKey.description: Allow to specify a target process key in which the process will be merged.

override.description: If set to true and if an undeployed model exist under the process key, the model will be erased. Otherwise, the method would have failed in such a case.

createdProcesses.description: processes key to model id if created processes

body.description: **_dryRun_** _boolean_</br>Default: ```false```</br>If set to ```true```, will return warning/errors messages you would get on import, without actually performing the importation.</br></br>**_failOnWarnings_** _boolean_</br>Default: ```true```</br>By default, process import is cancelled on errors or warnings detection. Set ```failOnWarnings``` to ```false``` if you want the import to fail only on errors detection.</br></br>**_usersMapping_** _Array of objects (Mapping)_</br>Users remapping to match logins from the imported files with specified logins.</br></br>**_groupsMapping_** _Array of objects (Mapping)_</br>Group remapping to match groupIds from the imported files with specified groupIds.</br></br>**_policiesMapping_** _Array of objects (Mapping)_</br>Policy remapping to match policy name from the imported files with specified policies name.</br></br>**_processName_** _string_</br>Name to set on the newly imported process. Importing on an already deployed process will take the name of existing process. New import without providing a processName will take the file name as process name.</br></br>**_processKey_** _string_</br>ProcessKey to set on the newly imported process. If a model already exists with same processKey, the import will fail.</br></br>**_override_** _boolean_</br>Default: ```false```</br>In case you precised the ```processKey``` to use, and a model (that is currently not deployed) already exists, it will be deleted before importing the new one.

dryRun.description: If set to `true`, will return warning/errors messages you would get on import, without actually performing the importation.

failOnWarnings.description: By default, process import is cancelled on errors or warnings detection. Set `failOnWarnings` to `false` if you want the import to fail only on errors detection.

usersMapping.description: Users remapping to match logins from the imported files with specified logins.

groupsMapping.description: Group remapping to match groupIds from the imported files with specified groupIds.

policiesMapping.description: Policy remapping to match policy name from the imported files with specified policies name.

processName.description: Name to set on the newly imported process. Importing on an already deployed process will take the name of existing process. New import without providing a processName will take the file name as process name.

processKey.description: ProcessKey to set on the newly imported process. If a model already exists with same processKey, the import will fail.

override.description: In case you precised the `processKey` to use, and a model (that is currently not deployed) already exists, it will be deleted before importing the new one.

files.description: Files to attach (Swagger can only handle one file while in reality you can send several files.)
Files to start the process (Swagger can only handle one file while in reality you can send several files.)

body.description: **_data_** _object_</br>Start values for process variables</br>**_identificator_** _string_</br>Process instance identificator</br>**_user_** _string_</br>Login to start process</br>

files.description: Files to attach (Swagger can only handle one file while in reality you can send several files.)

lockedBy.description: Task actor, currently locking the task

reassignmentCandidateUsers.description: Users that can be added to candidate users

reassignmentCandidateGroups.description: Groups that can be added to candidate groups

force.description: Force update even in case the task is currently locked by a user or resulting assignment contains no candidates.

quiet.description: Force update even in case users/groups are not found.

assignee.description: Task assignee (set it to an empty string in case you want to remove it)

quiet.description: Force update even in case users/groups are not found.
